pmos

Design — Load-bearing dogfooding verification (epic 260613-yyj)

Created using pmos‑toolkit

Load-bearing dogfooding verification in the SDLC pipeline

Epic: 260613-yyj · Route: skill · Plugin: pmos-toolkit · Defined: 2026-06-13

This is the cross-skill design contract for epic 260613-yyj (the role spec_doc: plays for feature epics). The two stories cite it by anchor; the per-skill plans turn its sections into testable tasks.

Summary

Make a load-bearing dogfooding task a first-class, verification-time step in the requirements→spec→plan→execute→verify pipeline. A dogfooding task actually uses the deliverable on a realistic, representative end-to-end task, scores the produced output against both objective and subjective (LLM-judge) criteria, and feeds failures back into fix tasks, auto-iterating until satisfied (cap 2, then ask). Mandatory for Tier 2 and Tier 3; optional for Tier 1.

Delivered by: a new shared substrate _shared/dogfooding.md (the canonical home), a /plan change that emits the task and asks the user to approve its scenario+criteria, and a /verify change that treats the task's verdict as a non-skippable gate and reconciles the iterate loop. /feature-sdlc inherits the behaviour for free — it already orchestrates /plan → /execute → /verify.

Problem — the gap today

/plan's final-verification task (TN, defined in plan/reference/plan-templates.md) is a smoke / does-it-render checklist: lint, type-check, unit + full test suite, an API smoke test, a Playwright walk-through that confirms UI elements render and the happy path works, a UX-polish checklist, and a wireframe diff. Every item answers "does the implementation work mechanically?"

None of them answer "is the thing the deliverable produces actually good and useful for a real task?" A new /research skill can pass every TN item — it runs, renders a report, no console errors — while the report it produces is shallow, mis-cited, or misses the question. The only way to catch that is to use the skill on a real topic and judge the output.

The maintainer repeatedly discovers this post-deployment: "use the new skill, have an independent LLM judge evaluate the report, fix the gaps, repeat." That utility validation — and the feedback loop it drives — belongs in the verification step, authored into the plan up front, not improvised after ship. This epic closes that gap.

Solution overview

  • Substrate _shared/dogfooding.md — the single home (§K one-fact-one-home) for: the task anatomy, the objective+subjective eval-criteria framework, the archetype catalog, the independent-judge contract, the iterate-until-satisfied protocol, the tier policy, and the plan-time approval gate. /plan and /verify cite it and state only per-skill deltas.
  • /plan emits a mandatory TN−1: Dogfood / Utility Verification task (immediately before TN Final Verification) for Tier 2/3 (optional for Tier 1), and asks the user to approve / edit the proposed dogfood scenario + criteria at plan time.
  • /verify treats the dogfood task's verdict as a load-bearing, non-skippable gate for Tier 2/3 (a Phase 7 hard gate, peer of the browser-evidence gate): an unsatisfied dogfood verdict caps the run at PASS-WITH-GAPS/FAIL. It reconciles the iterate loop and surfaces residuals.
  • /feature-sdlc — no change. It dispatches /plan//execute//verify, which now carry the behaviour. (Out of scope per D0.)

The substrate — _shared/dogfooding.md

Canonical home for the contract below. Lives in plugins/pmos-toolkit/skills/_shared/dogfooding.md and is cross-plugin-syncable via scripts/sync-shared.sh (intersection-only — only pmos-toolkit needs it in v1, so no bootstrap gap).

Definition & distinction from the TN smoke test

Dogfooding = exercise the actual deliverable on a realistic, representative end-to-end task and assess the utility and quality of what it produces — not merely that it ran without error.

TN Final Verification (existing)TN−1 Dogfood (new)
Does it render / compile / pass tests?Is the produced artifact actually good for a real task?
Mechanical correctness, no console errorsUtility, completeness, accuracy, fitness-for-purpose
Synthetic smoke inputs, happy + one error pathA real, representative end-to-end task the deliverable exists to serve
Self-checked by the implementerScored by an independent, blind judge against a declared rubric

The dogfood task is additive — both run. It is never a rename of, or substitute for, the existing render/lint checklist (D5).

Task anatomy

Every dogfood task declares, in its body: (1) the chosen archetype and a concrete scenario (the real task + representative input); (2) objective criteria; (3) subjective criteria (the judge rubric); (4) the independent-judge dispatch; (5) the iterate protocol and cap; (6) the verdict it writes for /verify to gate on.

Eval criteria — objective AND subjective (D3)

Every dogfood task must declare both kinds. A task with only one kind fails /plan's review check.

Objective criteria (measurable, deterministic where possible)

  • Pass/fail gates with exact commands and expected output.
  • Counts & rates: task-success rate, error rate, uncaught console errors = 0, broken-link count = 0, required elements present, citation/source count ≥ N.
  • Thresholds: latency / runtime bounds, output-size bounds.
  • Golden-sample / contract diffs where a reference output exists.

Subjective criteria (blind LLM-judge rubric)

  • A rubric of named dimensions appropriate to the deliverable. Example for a report: accuracy, completeness, relevance to the question, actionability, citation quality, clarity.
  • Each dimension scored with a verdict + one-line rationale grounded in the output (quote-grounded where applicable, per _shared/reviewer-protocol.md).
  • An overall satisfied / not-satisfied against an explicitly stated bar (e.g. "every dimension ≥ acceptable AND no dimension is a blocker").

Independent-judge contract (D4)

The subjective half is scored by a fresh subagent, blind to the author's intent — never the same context that produced the output (which would rubber-stamp it). It is given: the produced output (path or pasted), the rubric, and the original task the deliverable was asked to do. It returns a structured verdict (per-dimension verdict + rationale + overall satisfied flag + an enumerated gap list when not satisfied). It makes no edits — it scores and reports; the fix loop is driven separately. On platforms without subagents, fall back to a self-review framed "judge this as if seeing it for the first time," logged as a downgrade.

Archetype catalog (D8)

Not one rigid recipe — pick the archetype matching the deliverable; the scenario + criteria specialize from it.

DeliverableArchetypeHow it dogfoods
Skill / content generator (e.g. /research, /primer)Use → blind LLM-judgeInvoke the skill on a real topic; an independent judge scores the output against objective + rubric criteria; gaps → fix → re-run until satisfied.
Frontend / UI featureDev-server + browser-automation friction runStart the app; complete the real task in-browser (Playwright MCP); record success/error rate, console errors, friction points, confusion, bugs; gaps → fix → re-run.
CLI / toolReal-invocation against representative inputsRun on real inputs; compare to expected outcomes / golden samples; objective gates + judged usefulness of output.
Data pipeline / transformGolden-sample diff + spot-checkRun on a representative dataset; assert quality metrics; judge spot-checked records.
API / serviceScenario exerciseReal request sequences over primary + error journeys; contract + judged response fitness.

When the deliverable fits none, the task author writes a bespoke scenario satisfying the same dual-criteria + independent-judge + iterate contract.

Iterate-until-satisfied protocol (D2)

  1. Run the dogfood scenario; collect objective results + the judge's structured verdict.
  2. Satisfied (all objective gates green AND judge overall-satisfied) → record PASS verdict; done.
  3. Not satisfied and iteration count < 2 → enumerate concrete gaps, spawn fix tasks via /execute's existing discovered-work routing (no /execute skill change — D6), apply, then re-run the dogfood.
  4. Net-worse guard (mirror skill-eval): an iteration that fails more, or newly fails a previously-passing criterion, surfaces a "restore previous" option.
  5. Cap = 2 remediation iterations. Past it: AskUserQuestion — Accept residuals as known risk (recorded, re-checked by /verify, surfaced loudly) / Iterate manually / Restore previous (if net-worse) / Abort. No silent pass.
  6. Non-interactive (W14): the plan-time approval is deferred (use the recommended scenario); the loop auto-iterates to the cap; past the cap it accepts residuals and surfaces them loudly in the report and the /verify//complete-dev summary.

Tier policy (D1)

  • Tier 3 (feature): mandatory. At least one load-bearing dogfood task.
  • Tier 2 (enhancement): mandatory.
  • Tier 1 (bugfix): optional (recommended-skip) — the existing reduced TN smoke check stays the default; a dogfood task is offered when the fix warrants real-use validation.

Plan-time approval gate (D7)

/plan presents the proposed dogfood task — archetype, scenario, objective criteria, subjective rubric — via AskUserQuestion for the user to approve or edit before the plan finalizes (the maintainer explicitly asked for this). Recommended option = the auto-proposed scenario. Under --non-interactive the ask is deferred and the recommended scenario is used (W14), logged.

/plan changes (Story 260613-3ff)

  • plan/reference/plan-templates.md: add a TN−1: Dogfood / Utility Verification task template immediately before TN, citing _shared/dogfooding.md; emitted for Tier 2/3, offered for Tier 1.
  • plan/SKILL.md: Tier Gates note that T2/T3 plans must include the dogfood task; Phase that emits it selects an archetype and drafts the scenario+criteria; the plan-time approval AskUserQuestion (with a (Recommended) option).
  • Review checklist: a new structural check — "Dogfood task present & load-bearing for T2/T3, with BOTH objective and subjective criteria and a named independent judge"; exit-criteria addition.
  • tasks.yaml: the dogfood task serializes as a real task /execute runs (it is TN−1 in the breakdown).
  • Skill stays lean — detail lives in the substrate; /plan cites it (§K).

/verify changes (Story 260613-2m7)

  • New Phase 7 hard gate "Dogfood verdict" (peer of the browser-evidence gate): for Tier 2/3, the dogfood task must carry a satisfied verdict (objective gates green + judge satisfied). An unsatisfied/missing verdict means the run cannot be bare PASS → PASS-WITH-GAPS/FAIL, every gap enumerated.
  • Reconcile accepted_residuals[] from the iterate loop the same way skill-eval residuals are reconciled: a still-failing accepted residual is reported KNOWN / accepted (non-blocking, loud); a newly-failing objective gate blocks normally.
  • Non-skippable for T2/T3; cite _shared/dogfooding.md, state only the verify-side delta (§K).
  • Depends on Story 260613-3ff (the substrate + the task contract /verify gates on).

/feature-sdlc inheritance

No orchestrator change (D0). /feature-sdlc dispatches /plan (emits the task), /execute (runs it + discovered-work fix routing), and /verify (gates it). The substrate's tier rules + the children's behaviour cover the orchestrated path. Adding a dedicated orchestrator phase was considered and rejected as redundant.

Decisions (captured at define, 2026-06-13)

  • D0 — Scope: substrate + /plan + /verify; /feature-sdlc inherits free. Chosen over /plan-only (gate too soft — nothing enforces the dogfood verdict at verify time) and over also changing /feature-sdlc (the orchestrator phase is redundant; children carry it).
  • D1 — Tier policy: T2 + T3 mandatory, T1 optional. Matches the maintainer's stated preference.
  • D2 — Iterate loop: auto-iterate, cap 2, then ask; net-worse guard; non-interactive accepts-residuals-and-surfaces. Mirrors the skill-eval remediation loop the maintainer already knows.
  • D3 — Dual criteria: every dogfood task declares BOTH objective (measurable) and subjective (blind LLM-judge rubric) criteria. Explicit maintainer ask.
  • D4 — Independent judge: a fresh, blind subagent returns a structured verdict and makes no edits; never the producing context.
  • D5 — Additive, not a rename: the dogfood task is distinct from and additive to the existing TN smoke/render checklist; both run.
  • D6 — Placement & loop home: "TN−1: Dogfood / Utility Verification" immediately before TN; /execute runs it; the fix loop reuses /execute's existing discovered-work routing — no /execute skill change.
  • D7 — Approval gate: /plan asks the user to approve/edit the dogfood scenario + criteria at plan time.
  • D8 — Archetype catalog: skill→LLM-judge, frontend→Playwright-friction, CLI→real-invocation, data→golden-sample, API→scenario — pick by deliverable; bespoke when none fit.
  • D9 — Leanness (§K): the substrate holds the detail; /plan and /verify cite it and state only deltas. Skill bodies stay lean.

Stories

  • 260613-3ff — Author _shared/dogfooding.md substrate + /plan dogfood-task emission + approval gate + review checks. route: skill. deps: none. (The load-bearing story.)
  • 260613-2m7 — /verify load-bearing dogfood gate + iterate-loop reconciliation + residuals. route: skill. deps: 260613-3ff.

Split rationale: the substrate is co-designed with and born from /plan's needs (fused into one story); /verify is a thin consumer that depends on the substrate + the task contract being settled, and gets its own per-skill skill-eval (its own story). Both single-plugin (pmos-toolkit) — D17 satisfied.

Out of scope (v1)

  • A dedicated /feature-sdlc orchestrator phase (D0 — children carry it).
  • Any /execute skill change (D6 — discovered-work routing already exists).
  • A standalone /dogfood skill — the behaviour is a pipeline step, not a user-invoked skill in v1.
  • Cross-plugin propagation of the substrate (intersection-only sync; only pmos-toolkit needs it now).
  • Retrofitting dogfood verdicts onto already-shipped features.

Skill-authoring conformance

Both stories must satisfy the standing acceptance criteria: feature-sdlc/reference/skill-patterns.md §A–§L and the binary skill-eval.md rubric (floor 43/47), plus repo CLAUDE.md conventions (canonical skill path, manifest version-sync handled by /complete-dev only, non-interactive inline block byte-identical, (Recommended)/defer-only on every AskUserQuestion, §H–§L gates/flags/phases/one-home/dispatch). Release prerequisites (version bump, changelog, README, manifest sync, learnings header) belong under each story's spec/plan ## Release prerequisites only — never as /execute wave tasks.

pmos Source: docs/pmos/features/2026-06-13_dogfooding-verification/02_design.html · Created using pmos‑toolkit
